metadata.title: Improving Equilibrium Propagation Without Weight Symmetry Through Jacobian Homeostasis
organizations: Friedrich Miescher Institute for Biomedical Research
Faculty of Natural Sciences, University of Basel

sections.title: Generalized Equilibrium Propagation [1]
content: Goal: Local gradients for Neurobiology and Neuromorphics.

figures.description: Neural network architecture diagram showing weight symmetry notation between layers
Weight matrix visualization showing non-symmetric block structure

sections.title: Our contributions:
content: Disentangle the two sources of bias in non-symmetric EP
Extend holomorphic EP [2] to non symmetric networks
New homeostatic loss promotes Jacobian symmetry

sections.title: Accurate computation of the EP error vector

figures.description: Complex plane representation of the teaching signal phase
Graph showing cosine similarity of error vectors for different EP methods
Membrane potential oscillation over time showing raw and averaged signals
Online first mode estimation of membrane potential compared to true gradient
Density distribution of Jacobian symmetry for L2 layer showing decreasing symmetry
Network diagram illustrating Jacobian asymmetry effects across layers
Density distribution of Jacobian symmetry for L1 layer

sections.title: New homeostatic loss:
content: L_homeo := E[||Jf ε||^2 - ε^T Jf^2 ε]

sections.title: Benefits of the homeostatic loss on learning

figures.description: Validation error curves for Fashion MNIST and CIFAR-10 comparing models with and without homeostatic loss

tables.description: Comparison table of Top-1 and Top-5 accuracy on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet datasets

sections.title: References
content: [1] Scellier, et al. Generalization of equilibrium propagation to vector field dynamics. arXiv:1808.04873, 2018.[2] Laborieux and Zenke. Holomorphic equilibrium propagation computes exact gradients through finite size oscillations. NeurIPS 2022.
